response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist with genuinely relevant recent experience: he currently works on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow frameworks at Naver, which directly matches the role's AI-native baseline, and his Sociomantic work on micro-services handling millions of requests per second demonstrates real high-throughput systems experience. However, Python — a must-have listed as 'strong' — appears nowhere on the resume, his distributed-systems depth is several years old and in DLang, and there is little evidence of the technical-strategy and cross-functional stakeholder leadership expected at Staff level, where his Staff title is only about a year old.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-native work: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks at Naver
Proven high-throughput experience: low-latency micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong TypeScript/Node.js plus disciplined engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume, despite 'strong Python' being a must-have
Little demonstrated technical-strategy ownership or cross-functional stakeholder management at Staff scope
High-throughput distributed-systems experience is dated (2018-2020) and in DLang rather than the role's stack

key_factors.factor: AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling experience
